categories.name: Agent Depth & Specialization

evidence.claim: Twenty-five agents are mapped to canonical skills, knowledge, templates and evidence requirements.
Support agents execute as dependency-ordered workflow nodes rather than remaining routing metadata.

open_gaps: Some agent definitions still lack direct operator-facing command entrypoints.

categories.name: Skill Coverage & Breadth

evidence.claim: The current index contains a broad skill catalogue with enforced deep-procedure coverage.
Canonical procedure headings are validated, but many skills remain grouped rather than independently packaged.

open_gaps: Executable high-value skills are not consistently self-contained packages.
Reference depth per skill remains uneven.

categories.name: Script & Execution Tooling

evidence.claim: The repository contains tested execution helpers, but the callable SEO command surface remains materially smaller than Claude SEO.
The runtime can orchestrate tools safely, but orchestration depth is ahead of direct data-acquisition and analysis command depth.

open_gaps: No unified seoctl command surface.
Major gaps remain in GSC, GA4, CrUX history, content intelligence, authority and media tooling.

evidence.claim: The runtime uses a bounded multi-agent DAG with explicit dependency ordering.
Agent outputs are schema validated and arbitrary prose is not wrapped as success.
Nodes, LLM calls, corrections, runtime and optional cost are bounded before execution.

categories.name: Data Integrations & Live APIs

evidence.claim: The adapter layer is broad and normalized, but most sources depend on supplied exports.
MCP capability governance exists, but documented providers are not all installable, authenticated and smoke-tested integrations.

open_gaps: Production GSC and GA4 connectors.
Bing and IndexNow integration.
Installable optional paid-provider packs.
Authorized live smoke-test evidence.

categories.name: Knowledge & Reference Depth

evidence.claim: Several canonical rubrics and policy references are high quality and evidence labeled.
The source hierarchy is strong, but coverage is not yet comprehensive across every executable capability.

open_gaps: Reference coverage is materially thinner than the comparator.
Freshness enforcement is not yet complete across all time-sensitive references.

categories.name: Test Coverage & CI/CD

evidence.claim: CI runs on Windows and Ubuntu with Python 3.11 and 3.13 and enforces repository, schema, tracer and pytest gates.
The post-remediation suite exceeded two hundred tests with cross-platform verification.

open_gaps: No enforced coverage threshold.
No secret scan, dependency audit, SBOM, typing or lint gate.
README lacks evidence-linked status badges.

categories.name: Content Generation Capability

evidence.claim: The FLOW layer is evidence grounded and integrated.
Public writing has quality and claims controls, but the production prompt and analysis surface remains narrow.

open_gaps: No modular content intelligence command family.
No optional image-generation pack.
No deterministic content verification and entity-analysis suite comparable to Claude SEO.

categories.name: Documentation & Onboarding

evidence.claim: The repository now includes Start Here guidance and executable CLI examples.
A contribution standard exists, but install-to-success onboarding and generated command documentation remain incomplete.

open_gaps: No dedicated installation and quick-start journey.
No generated command reference.
Architecture and integration diagrams are incomplete.
No troubleshooting guide.

categories.name: Community, Maturity & Ecosystem

evidence.claim: The repository now has substantive version history and an Unreleased section.
The project still lacks public release tags, issue templates, contributors metadata, CODEOWNERS and packaged distribution paths.

open_gaps: No signed or checksummed public release.
No CONTRIBUTORS.md or CITATION.cff.
No issue and PR templates.
No marketplace or installable platform packages.

notes: The supplied 73/100 score was not mathematically reproducible from its category table and weights.
This baseline uses an explicit weighted formula and does not count file quantity as capability maturity.
Runtime architecture improvements are included; tooling, integrations, references and ecosystem remain the primary gaps.
